title: Rename 'Voice Code' to 'Untethered' in all user-facing UI
description:
## Problem

The product name is **Untethered**, but 'Voice Code' and 'VoiceCodeMobile' still appear in several user-facing locations.

## User-Facing Instances Found

### Launch Screen (most visible)
- **LaunchScreen.storyboard**: Shows 'VoiceCodeMobile' as the app title during launch
  - Path: `frontend/ios/VoiceCodeMobile/LaunchScreen.storyboard`

### App Configuration  
- **app.json**: `displayName` is 'Voice Code'
  - Path: `frontend/app.json`

### iOS Permission Dialogs
- **Info.plist**: Three permission descriptions mention 'Voice Code':
  - `NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`: 'Voice Code needs microphone access...'
  - `NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`: 'Voice Code needs speech recognition...'
  - `NSCameraUsageDescription`: 'Voice Code needs camera access...'
  - Path: `frontend/ios/VoiceCodeMobile/Info.plist`

### In-App Screens
- **auth.cljs**: Login screen header shows 'Voice Code'
  - Path: `frontend/src/voice_code/views/auth.cljs` (initial-setup-header function)
- **directory_list.cljs**: Welcome/first-run screen shows 'Welcome to Voice Code'
  - Path: `frontend/src/voice_code/views/directory_list.cljs` (configure-server-state function)

## Scope

Replace all user-visible instances of 'Voice Code' / 'VoiceCodeMobile' with 'Untethered'.

Internal code (namespaces, file paths, variable names) can retain 'voice-code' naming since users don't see these.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Launch screen shows 'Untethered'
- [ ] app.json displayName is 'Untethered'  
- [ ] All iOS permission dialogs say 'Untethered needs...'
- [ ] Auth/login screen shows 'Untethered'
- [ ] Welcome/first-run screen shows 'Welcome to Untethered'
- [ ] Test on iOS simulator to verify all changes
notes:
Completed renaming all user-facing 'Voice Code' instances to 'Untethered':

Changes made:
- frontend/ios/VoiceCodeMobile/LaunchScreen.storyboard: Changed 'VoiceCodeMobile' to 'Untethered', removed 'Powered by React Native'
- frontend/app.json: Changed displayName from 'Voice Code' to 'Untethered'  
- frontend/ios/VoiceCodeMobile/Info.plist: Updated 3 permission descriptions from 'Voice Code needs...' to 'Untethered needs...'
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/auth.cljs: Changed initial-setup-header title to 'Untethered'
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/directory_list.cljs: Changed welcome text to 'Welcome to Untethered'

Verification: All iOS tests pass (12 tests), iOS build succeeds, verified Info.plist in built .app bundle shows 'Untethered'

title: Server timestamp tracking for messages
description:
iOS CDMessage tracks both local timestamp and serverTimestamp separately. RN only tracks a single timestamp field.

iOS Reference: ios/VoiceCode/Models/CDMessage+CoreDataClass.swift
- timestamp (Date) - local timestamp when message created/received
- serverTimestamp (Date?) - timestamp from backend (optional)

Purpose:
- Server timestamp enables accurate message ordering after sync
- Local timestamp used for display relative times
- Helps with delta sync and message deduplication

Current RN state:
- Messages have single :timestamp field
- No distinction between local and server time

RN Implementation needed:
- Add :server-timestamp field to message schema
- Parse server timestamp from backend responses
- Use server timestamp for ordering when available

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/db.cljs - add server-timestamp to message schema
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - parse server timestamp

Acceptance criteria:
- Messages track both local and server timestamps
- Message ordering uses server timestamp when available
- Backward compatible with messages without server timestamp

title: [UI Bug] Navigation crash - Cannot read property navigate of undefined
description:

title: Message send failure error status display
description:
## Problem

When a user sends a message that fails (backend returns success=false), the message stays showing 'Sending...' indefinitely instead of showing an error indicator.

## Expected Behavior (iOS parity)

In the iOS app, when a message fails to send:
1. The message shows an error icon (exclamationmark.triangle.fill in red)
2. The message status transitions from 'sending' to 'error'
3. The user can clearly see that their message didn't go through

## Current RN Behavior

In the React Native app:
1. The message shows 'Sending...' indicator
2. When response fails, a banner error is shown (`:ui/current-error`)
3. BUT the message itself never transitions to ':status :error'
4. The message bubble continues showing 'Sending...' indefinitely

## Root Cause

In `events/websocket.cljs` `:ws/handle-response`:
- On success: adds new assistant message with `:status :confirmed`
- On failure: only sets `:ui/current-error` and unlocks session
- Missing: updating the original user message to `:status :error`

## Fix Required

1. Update `:ws/handle-response` to mark the last user message as `:status :error` on failure
2. Update `conversation.cljs` `message-bubble` to display error icon when `:status :error`

## Files to Modify

- `frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs`
- `frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] When a message send fails, the message shows red error icon
- [ ] Error banner still displays for overall error context
- [ ] User can distinguish between 'sending' and 'failed' states
- [ ] Tests added for error status transition
close_reason: Implementation verified: ws/handle-response marks message as :error on failure, message-bubble displays red Failed to send indicator, tests exist in handle-response-error-marks-message-test

title: Draft message persistence
description:
iOS uses DraftManager to persist draft messages per-session to UserDefaults with 300ms debounce. RN drafts stored only in volatile app-db.

**iOS DraftManager Features:**
- Per-session draft storage (session ID -> text)
- Debounced writes to UserDefaults (300ms)
- save(), get(), clear(), cleanup() lifecycle
- Survives app restarts and background

**Current RN State:**
- Drafts in app-db under :ui :drafts
- Lost on app restart
- No debouncing on writes

**Implementation:**
1. Add draft persistence to AsyncStorage
2. Debounce writes (300ms) to reduce I/O
3. Load drafts on app init
4. Clear draft on message send
5. Cleanup stale drafts periodically

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Add draft persistence
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add draft save effect

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Draft text survives app restart
- [ ] Drafts debounced to reduce I/O
- [ ] Draft cleared after successful send
- [ ] Old drafts for deleted sessions cleaned up
close_reason: Closed

- markCommandUsed() called when executing a command
- MRU data stored in UserDefaults

The RN command_menu.cljs displays commands in the order received from the backend without any sorting.

**Implementation:**
1. Add :commands/mru key to app-db (map of command-id -> timestamp)
2. Add event :commands/mark-used to update MRU timestamp
3. Add subscription :commands/sorted-for-directory that applies MRU sorting
4. Persist MRU data to AsyncStorage
5. Call mark-used when executing a command

**Reference:** ios/VoiceCode/Utils/CommandSorter.swift

title: AppState-based UI update suspension
description:
iOS DirectoryListView suspends cache updates when app is backgrounded to prevent watchdog kills. This prevents expensive re-renders when app is not visible.

## iOS Implementation
DirectoryListView.swift has scene phase awareness:
- Uses `@Environment(\.scenePhase)` to detect active/inactive/background
- Suspends debounced cache updates when not in foreground
- Prevents watchdog kills from expensive background renders

## Current RN State
- No AppState handling for UI updates
- Directory list may continue expensive renders when backgrounded
- Could cause performance issues or battery drain

## Acceptance Criteria

title: [UI Bug] Unsafe navigation calls - missing nil checks
description:
## Problem
Multiple views call .navigate on the navigation prop without checking if it exists.

## Affected Files
- directory_list.cljs: settings-button uses (.navigate navigation ...) without guard
- session_list.cljs: commands-button and render-item use (.navigate navigation ...) without guard  
- command_menu.cljs: command on-press handlers use (.navigate navigation ...) without guard

## Root Cause
The navigation prop can be undefined during:
- Hot reload (stale references)
- Component mounting race conditions
- Module loading

## Fix
Wrap all .navigate calls:
```clojure
(when navigation
  (.navigate navigation "ScreenName" params))
```

Or use the safe navigate! function from voice-code.views.core:
```clojure
(voice-code.views.core/navigate! "ScreenName" params)
```

## Related
This is the root cause of VCMOB-1i4 (navigation crash)
close_reason: Closed

title: QR Scanner 'Grant Permission' button unresponsive - overlay blocks touch events
description:
## Summary

The 'Grant Permission' button on the QR Scanner screen does nothing when tapped. Users cannot grant camera permission, blocking the authentication flow.

## Root Cause

In `frontend/src/voice_code/qr_scanner.cljs`, the `scanner-overlay` component is always rendered regardless of permission state (lines 286-289):

```clojure
[:> rn/View {:style {:flex 1 :background-color "#000000"}}
 [:f> scanner-camera]
 [scanner-overlay]      ;; <-- ALWAYS rendered
 [error-banner]]
```

The overlay has `position: absolute` covering the entire screen (top/left/right/bottom: 0), intercepting all touch events and preventing the 'Grant Permission' button underneath from receiving taps.

## Expected Behavior

The overlay should only render when camera permission is granted and the camera is active - not during the permission request flow.

## Fix

Conditionally render `scanner-overlay` only when camera has permission:

```clojure
[:> rn/View {:style {:flex 1 :background-color "#000000"}}
 [:f> scanner-camera]
 (when has-permission [scanner-overlay])  ;; Only when camera active
 [error-banner]]
```

Note: This requires lifting permission state up or passing it as a prop, since `has-permission` is currently only available inside the `scanner-camera` component's hook.

## Impact

- **Severity**: Blocks new user authentication via QR code
- **Workaround**: None from QR scanner; user must manually enter server details in Settings
close_reason: Fixed by conditionally rendering scanner overlay only when camera has permission AND device is available. The camera-ready? atom tracks state from inside scanner-camera hook. qr-scanner-view only renders scanner-overlay when @camera-ready? is true. Commit: 4128d48c. Verified with 206 passing unit tests.

title: Optimistic message reconciliation - update sending status to confirmed
description:
**Current behavior:** When user sends a prompt, RN creates an optimistic message with `:status :sending`. When server confirms via session_history, RN adds the confirmed message but doesn't update the optimistic one's status.

**iOS behavior:** SessionSyncManager.swift reconciles optimistic messages by matching (sessionId, role, text) and updates status from `.sending` to `.confirmed`, also updating the ID to match backend's UUID.

**Impact:** Without reconciliation, users may see duplicate messages (one :sending, one :confirmed) or messages stuck in :sending state.

**Implementation:**
1. In `db/merge-messages`, when a new message matches an existing message by (session-id, role, text), update the existing message's status to :confirmed and its ID to the backend ID
2. Or implement a separate reconciliation step in handle-history

**Reference:** ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift:265-285 (reconcileMessage function)
close_reason: Implemented optimistic message reconciliation in db/merge-messages. Added reconcile-optimistic-message helper that matches messages by (session-id, role, text) for user messages with :sending status. When match found: updates ID to server ID, sets status to :confirmed. Reference: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift:296-312. Tests: 226 passed, 1028 assertions, 0 failures.

title: Session message list sometimes empty until manual refresh - possible race condition
description:
## Bug Report

### Summary
When selecting/opening a session, the message list sometimes shows as empty. Messages only appear after manually hitting the refresh button.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Connect to backend successfully
2. Select a session from the session list
3. **Expected**: Messages load and display
4. **Actual**: Message list appears empty (intermittent)
5. Hit refresh button manually
6. Messages now appear

### Behavior
- Intermittent - doesn't happen every time
- Suggests race condition between navigation and data loading

### Investigation Focus
**IMPORTANT**: Verify we are NOT using timing hacks. Check for:
- `setTimeout` / `js/setTimeout` delays to "wait for data"
- `Thread/sleep` or similar on backend
- Any hardcoded delays as workarounds

**Should be using**:
- Proper event/signal flow (re-frame events completing before UI updates)
- Loading states that wait for actual data arrival
- Subscription to session history completing before displaying

### Likely Areas
- `subscribe` message to backend and `session_history` response handling
- Navigation to Conversation screen vs data readiness
- Re-frame subscription for messages not populated when view renders

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Messages always display when opening a session (no manual refresh needed)
- [ ] No sleep/setTimeout hacks in the flow
- [ ] Proper loading state shown while fetching messages
- [ ] Event-driven data flow verified
close_reason: Fixed subscription called outside reactive context - created header-right-buttons-wrapper component

title: Infer Name button returns 'Unknown message type: infer_name' error
description:
## Bug Report

### Summary
When clicking the "Infer Name" button (likely in Session Info view), the frontend receives an error: "Unknown message type: infer_name"

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a session
2. Navigate to Session Info
3. Click "Infer Name" button
4. **Result**: Error displayed "Unknown message type: infer_name"

### Backend Log Evidence
Message reaches the backend successfully but no handler exists:
```
INFO: === Received message === {:type infer_name, :type-class java.lang.String, :type-bytes [105 110 102 101 114 95 110 97 109 101]}
WARNING: Unknown message type {:type infer_name}
```

### Root Cause
Backend WebSocket message dispatch does not have a handler for `infer_name` message type. The frontend is sending a valid message, but backend support was never implemented.

### Fix Required
Add `infer_name` message handler to backend WebSocket protocol:
- Location: `backend/src/voice_code/websocket.clj` (or similar)
- Handler should infer a session name from conversation context
- Return inferred name to frontend

### Related
- Session info UI was added in commit deb02f5c
- Backend message handlers in `voice-code.websocket` namespace
close_reason: Fixed infer_name to infer_session_name message type and added message-text parameter

title: Delta sync message merging - append new messages instead of replacing

## Problem

- Backend returns only newer messages
- handle-history REPLACES messages[:session-id] entirely
- Existing messages are lost

## Expected Behavior
- If last_message_id was sent, APPEND returned messages to existing
- If no last_message_id (fresh load), REPLACE as current
- Deduplicate by message ID to prevent duplicates

## iOS Implementation
SessionSyncManager deduplicates by UUID before adding messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Track whether subscribe was delta or full request
- [ ] Merge new messages with existing on delta sync
- [ ] Deduplicate by message ID
- [ ] Preserve message order (chronological)

title: QR code scanning rejects valid backend QR codes
description:
## Bug Description

When scanning the QR code generated by the backend (known working code) during initial connection setup, the React Native app shows an error:

```
Invalid QR code. Expected voice-ce authenticated code.
```

Note: The error message appears to have a typo ('voice-ce' instead of 'voice-code'), which may indicate string truncation or a typo in the validation code.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start the backend server (known working QR generation)
2. Open the React Native app
3. Navigate to Settings or initial connection setup
4. Attempt to scan the backend-generated QR code
5. Observe the error message

## Expected Behavior

The app should successfully parse the QR code and establish connection with the backend.

## Actual Behavior

App rejects the QR code with an invalid format error.

## Investigation Notes

- Check QR code validation logic in frontend
- Verify expected QR format matches what backend generates
- The 'voice-ce' typo suggests looking for string literals in validation code

## Testing Requirements

- Manual testing with actual QR code scanning before marking complete
- Automated tests for QR parsing logic
- Test both valid and invalid QR code formats
notes: Root cause identified: RN QR parser expected URL format (voice-code://connect?...) but backend generates raw API keys (untethered-...). iOS implementation correctly validates raw API keys. Fix: Updated parse-qr-code in qr_scanner.cljs to validate raw API keys matching iOS KeychainManager.isValidAPIKeyFormat() logic. Updated tests to match new behavior. Reference: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/KeychainManager.swift:53, ios/VoiceCode/Views/QRScannerView.swift:239
close_reason: Fixed: Changed parse-qr-code to validate raw API keys (untethered-<32 hex chars>) instead of URL format. Now matches iOS KeychainManager.isValidAPIKeyFormat() behavior exactly. Tests updated and passing (207 tests, 912 assertions). REPL verification confirms valid keys accepted, invalid formats rejected.

title: [UI Bug] SessionList shows 'No Sessions' despite data being available in subscription
description:
## Description
SessionList and Conversation views show empty state despite subscriptions containing data.

## Affected Views
1. **SessionList** - Shows 'No Sessions' when subscription has 20 sessions
2. **Conversation** - Shows 'Start a Conversation' when subscription has 54 messages

## Root Cause
Likely Reagent reactivity issue with `r/reactify-component` wrapper used for React Navigation screens. The reactive context may not be properly established when components are created through React Navigation.

## Additional Finding
Messages in the subscription have nil `:text` and `:role` fields - possible data parsing issue from WebSocket message handling.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Authenticate via REPL
2. Navigate to SessionList or Conversation
3. Observe empty state despite subscription returning data

## Technical Analysis
```clojure
;; Subscription returns correct data:
@(rf/subscribe [:sessions/for-directory "/path"]) ;; => 20 sessions
@(rf/subscribe [:messages/for-session id]) ;; => 54 messages

;; But components check (empty? sessions) or (empty? messages) => true
```

## Impact
P1 - Core functionality broken. Users cannot see sessions or messages.
close_reason: Fixed by converting all view components to Form-2 pattern. The root cause was subscriptions being read in the outer function scope of components wrapped with r/reactify-component for React Navigation. Moving subscriptions into the inner render function establishes proper Reagent reactive context.

title: Code block filtering for TTS read aloud
description:
## Problem

When reading assistant messages aloud with TTS, raw code blocks may be spoken, creating a poor audio experience.

## iOS Reference

ios/VoiceCode/Utilities/TextProcessor.swift uses `removeCodeBlocks()` before passing text to TTS:
- Removes fenced code blocks (\`\`\`...\`\`\`)
- Removes inline code (\`...\`)
- Preserves natural language for better TTS output

- [ ] Natural language surrounding code is preserved
notes:
This feature is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED. Code review confirms:

The :voice/speak-response event handler in voice.cljs already calls (remove-code-blocks text) before passing to TTS:

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-fx
 :voice/speak-response
 (fn [{:keys [db]} [_ text working-directory]]
   (let [selected-voice-id (get-in db [:settings :voice-identifier])
         premium-voices (get-premium-voices (get-in db [:ui :available-voices] []))
         resolved-voice-id (resolve-voice-identifier selected-voice-id premium-voices working-directory)
         ;; Process text to remove code blocks before TTS
         processed-text (remove-code-blocks text)]
     {:voice/speak {:text processed-text :voice-id resolved-voice-id}})))
```

The remove-code-blocks function:
- Replaces fenced code blocks with '[code block]' spoken placeholder
- Removes inline code backticks but keeps content
- Cleans up multiple newlines

title: Projects view flashes to login page repeatedly - missing set-authenticated event handler
description:
## Bug Report

### Summary
On the Projects view, the UI rapidly flashes between the login page and projects list approximately once per second, as if in a connection retry loop.

### Console Error
```
re-frame: no :event handler registered for:
{"ns":"connection","name":"set-authenticated","fqn":"connection/set-authenticated","_hash":-128884674,"cljs$lang$protocol_mask$partition0$":2153775105,"cljs$lang$protocol_mask$partition1$":4096}
```

### Root Cause
The `:connection/set-authenticated` re-frame event handler is not registered. This causes:
1. Connection/auth flow dispatches `:connection/set-authenticated`
2. re-frame throws error - no handler registered
3. Auth state remains incomplete/fails
4. App shows login screen
5. Reconnection attempts, dispatches event again
6. Cycle repeats → UI flashing

### Call Stack (unsymbolicated)
- `_construct` (main.jsbundle:29460:106)
- `Wrapper` (main.jsbundle:29434:64)
- `_callSuper` (main.jsbundle:29279:142)
- `SyntheticError` (main.jsbundle:29288:25)
- `reactConsoleErrorHandler` (main.jsbundle:29400:33)
- `registerError` (main.jsbundle:29833:37)
- `cljs.core.js:13553:14`

### Fix Required
Register the `:connection/set-authenticated` event handler in the events namespace:
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
 :connection/set-authenticated
 (fn [db [_ authenticated?]]
   (assoc-in db [:connection :authenticated?] authenticated?)))
```

### Environment
- Physical iPhone via `make rn-deploy-device`
- Debug build

### Notes
- Source maps not available (shows "⚠ Source Map" warning)
- Related to connection/auth state management refactoring
close_reason: Added :connection/set-authenticated event handler in events/core.cljs. Added test in events_test.cljs. All tests pass (165 tests, 658 assertions).

title: Rebase react-native branch on local main and restart backend

2. **Restart backend**: After successful rebase, run `make backend-restart` from the root directory
3. **Mandatory code review via subagent**: After rebase completion, spawn a subagent to perform a code review of the rebase changes with special attention to:
   - Any merge conflicts that were resolved
   - Whether conflict resolutions preserved intended behavior
   - Any code that looks like it may have been incorrectly merged

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Branch rebased on local main (verify with `git log --oneline main..HEAD`)
- [ ] Backend server restarted via `make backend-restart`
- [ ] Subagent code review completed with findings documented
- [ ] Any merge conflict resolutions reviewed and validated

title: [UI Bug] DirectoryList session count not displayed - clj->js key conversion issue
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the app
2. View the Projects (DirectoryList) screen
3. Observe each directory item shows 'sessions' without a number

## Expected Behavior
Each directory item should show the session count, e.g., '3 sessions' or '1 session'

## Actual Behavior
Each directory item just shows 'sessions' with no count

## Screenshot
/tmp/test-screenshot-1.png

## Root Cause
In directory_list.cljs, the render-item function uses:
```clojure
:session-count (.-sessionCount item)
```

But `clj->js` converts `{:session-count 5}` to JavaScript object with key `"session-count"` (preserves kebab-case as string), NOT `sessionCount`.

## Fix
Change the property access from:
```clojure
:session-count (.-sessionCount item)
:last-modified (.-lastModified item)
```
to:
```clojure
:session-count (aget item "session-count")
:last-modified (aget item "last-modified")
```

Or use `:keywordize-keys` option when converting.

## Additional Context
- File: frontend/src/voice_code/views/directory_list.cljs
- Function: directory-list-view, render-item callback
- Same issue likely affects last-modified display
close_reason: Closed

title: [UI Bug] Screen frozen on CommandExecution - navigation/auth state changes don't trigger re-render
description:
## Description
The UI becomes completely frozen on the CommandExecution screen and does not respond to any navigation or authentication state changes.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. App is showing CommandExecution screen (from previous command)
2. Authenticate via REPL
3. Attempt to navigate to any screen via `navigate!` or `.reset`
4. Navigation state changes (verified via `.getState`) but UI doesn't update

## Observed Behavior
- Navigation state shows `{:index 2, :routes [DirectoryList, Settings, DirectoryList]}`
- UI continues to show CommandExecution screen (not in routes array!)
- Setting `authenticated? false` doesn't show auth-view
- `.reset` with new routes doesn't update display

## Expected Behavior
- Navigation should update displayed screen
- Auth state change should switch between auth-view and NavigationContainer

## Technical Details
- nav-ref.getState() returns correct state
- re-frame subscriptions return correct values
- React component tree not re-rendering
- re-frame warning visible: "no :event handler registered for: (...)"

## Impact
P0 - App unusable when this state occurs. Requires app restart to recover.
close_reason: Fixed by converting all view components to Form-2 pattern (inner render function) so subscriptions are read inside a reactive context. This ensures React Navigation screens properly re-render when re-frame subscriptions change.

title: Delta sync last_message_id persistence
description:

Reference: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift lines 1142-1168
notes:
Implemented delta sync last_message_id persistence:

1. Modified :persistence/load-messages effect to support on-complete callback
2. Modified :sessions/select event to:
   - Check if messages exist in memory
   - If yes: dispatch :session/subscribe directly 
   - If no: load messages from persistence first, then dispatch :session/subscribe

This enables efficient delta sync after app restart by loading persisted messages before subscribing, so the last_message_id is available for the subscribe request.

Files modified:
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Added on-complete callback to load-messages effect
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Modified sessions/select to load from persistence first

title: Tap to copy error message fails - clipboard.setString is not a function
description:
## Bug Report

### Summary
When tapping an error message to copy it (using the "Tap to copy" feature), an uncaught error occurs: "clipboard.setString is not a function (it is undefined)"

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Trigger any error that displays in the console/error UI
2. Tap on the error message to copy it
3. **Result**: Uncaught Error - clipboard.setString is not a function (it is undefined)

### Root Cause
The clipboard module is not properly imported or linked:
- `@react-native-clipboard/clipboard` may not be installed
- Or native module not linked (pod install needed)
- Or wrong import path being used

### Fix Required
1. Verify `@react-native-clipboard/clipboard` is in package.json
2. Run `cd frontend/ios && pod install`
3. Check import statement in ClojureScript:
   ```clojure
   ;; Should be something like:
   (def Clipboard (.-default (js/require "@react-native-clipboard/clipboard")))
   (.setString Clipboard "text to copy")
   ```

title: React Navigation component naming warnings
description:
## Problem

React Navigation logs warnings about component names not starting with uppercase letters:

```
Got a component with the name 'voice_code.views.debug_logs.debug_logs_view' for the screen 'DebugLogs'. React Components must start with an uppercase letter. If you're passing a regular function and not a component, pass it as children to 'Screen' instead. Otherwise capitalize your component's name.
```

This affects multiple view files where Reagent components are being passed directly to React Navigation.

## Root Cause

Reagent components created with `defn` have snake_case names in ClojureScript which don't follow React's PascalCase naming convention for components. The warning comes from React Navigation checking the `.name` property of functions, which is set at definition time and cannot be changed.

## Partial Fix Applied

Added `reactify-with-name` helper in core.cljs that sets `displayName` on React components. This helps with:
- React DevTools component names
- Error stack traces
- Component identification in React profiler

However, React Navigation's warning uses `.name` not `.displayName`, so the warnings persist.

## Impact

**Low** - These are cosmetic warnings only. The app functions correctly. The warnings appear in Debug Logs but don't affect user experience.

## Possible Full Fixes (Future)

1. Patch React Navigation to check `displayName` first
2. Use JavaScript wrapper functions with proper names
3. Create a custom Babel plugin to rename compiled functions
4. Accept the warnings as a known ClojureScript/RN integration limitation

## Files Modified

- frontend/src/voice_code/views/core.cljs - Added `reactify-with-name` helper

title: Maximum call stack size exceeded error on first connection with API key
description:
## Bug Report

### Summary
When entering API key to connect for the first time on physical device with incorrect server host, a console error appears: "Maximum call stack size exceeded (native stack depth)"

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Fresh app install on physical iPhone
2. Enter API key manually (not QR scan)
3. Leave server host as default (pointing to wrong/unreachable server)
4. Attempt to connect
5. **Result**: Console shows "Maximum call stack size exceeded (native stack depth)" error

### Resolution
After changing the host to the correct server address, connection works normally.

### Root Cause
The stack overflow appears to be triggered by error handling when connection fails to wrong server. Possibly:
- Retry logic looping infinitely
- Error handler triggering reconnect which fails and triggers error handler again
- Re-frame event/subscription cycle on connection error

### Environment
- Device: Physical iPhone
- App: React Native debug build via `make rn-deploy-device`

### Notes
- Error console shows "triangle Source Map" icon indicating source maps not available
- May be debug build artifact - needs verification in release build
- Error handling for connection failures should fail gracefully, not stack overflow

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Connecting to wrong/unreachable server shows user-friendly error message
- [ ] No stack overflow or infinite loop on connection failure
- [ ] Verify behavior in both debug and release builds
close_reason: Added try-catch in websocket connect and was-connected? flag to prevent reconnection loops on initial connection failure

**Current RN State:**

- [ ] Implement onEndReached handler in message FlatList
- [ ] Load messages in batches (e.g., 50 at a time)
- [ ] Request older messages from backend when scrolling up
- [ ] Show loading indicator while fetching
- [ ] Maintain scroll position when prepending messages

title: Message acknowledgment for delivery tracking
description:

## iOS Implementation

## Current RN State

## Protocol (from STANDARDS.md)
```json
{"type": "message_ack", "message_id": "<unique-message-id>"}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Call :ws/send-message-ack from :ws/handle-response for successful responses
- [ ] Call :ws/send-message-ack from :ws/handle-replay for replayed messages
- [ ] Verify acks sent via network inspection
